title: Deep generative classification of blood cell morphology

authors.affiliation: 1 Department of Applied Mathematics and Theoretical Physics, University of Cambridge, Cambridge, UK
2 Department of Laboratory Medicine, UZ Leuven, Leuven, Belgium
3 Department of Cardiovascular Sciences, Center for Molecular and Vascular Biology, University of Leuven, Leuven, Belgium

10 Department of Medicine, University of Cambridge, Cambridge, UK

process: Input image x₀ is encoded into a latent space using an encoder ℰ
Gaussian noise ε ~ N(0,I) is added to create a noisy latent representation zₜ
The noisy representation is fed through a diffusion model for each possible class condition c
The model predicts the noise ε_θ for each condition
Classification decision is made by selecting the class that minimizes the error between predicted noise and true noise

key_insights: Blood cell morphology is described as complex and requiring expert visual description
The task of morphological characterization is open-ended and lacks definitive ground truth
Simple blood counts of cell class frequencies don't capture the richness of morphological appearances
